item.title: Norway arrests three brothers over U.S. embassy bombing in Oslo
item.description:
Hatlo said police have not ruled out further arrests and are investigating whether the attack was carried out at the behest of a state actor. "That is quite natural given the target of the action, the American embassy, and the security policy situation the world is in today," he said.
Other hypotheses include possible links to criminal networks or personal motives, Hatlo added, stressing that...

item.title: Kilauea's 1,000-foot lava fountains force closures on Big Island
item.description: Kilauea, one of the world's most active volcanoes, has been erupting intermittently since December 2024, with episodes ranging from a few hours to several days. The eruption has drawn large numbers of visitors to the park, where the volcanic activity is visible from open viewing areas along the caldera rim.

item.title: Trump says 'practically nothing left' to target in Iran
item.description: The conflicting signals underscore a deepening uncertainty about when or how the conflict might conclude. Even as Trump has publicly suggested the operation has largely achieved its objectives, U.S. and Israeli officials have offered no internal directive on a timeline for ending hostilities, according to reporting by Moneycontrol. A Reuters analysis found that Iran is banking on outlasting its...

item.title: IRGC claims strikes on ships in Strait of Hormuz, demands transit approval
item.description:
The attacks bring the total number of vessels targeted since the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran began on February 28 to at least 14, with the UKMTO logging 13 confirmed assaults through Wednesday. The incidents came hours after the U.S. military announced it had destroyed 16 Iranian minelaying vessels near the strait.
Maritime traffic through the passage, which normally carries roughly a fifth...

item.title: IEA confirms largest-ever oil reserve release at 400M barrels
item.description:
The proposed release would be spread over at least two months, with participating countries given up to 90 days to distribute the volume, according to Spain's energy minister Sara Aagesen. IEA member countries collectively hold around 1.2 billion barrels in strategic reserves, with an additional 600 million barrels in government-mandated industry stocks.
The United States had rebuilt its...

item.title: White House says Trump will accept Iran school strike probe findings
item.description: The strike occurred on the morning of February 28, as children were attending lessons at the school in Minab, near the strategically important Strait of Hormuz. Iranian authorities have reported between 150 and 180 deaths, with many victims schoolgirls between the ages of seven and twelve who were trapped when the roof collapsed. Iran's education ministry put the toll at 168. UNESCO described...

item.title: Anthropic launches policy institute amid Pentagon legal battle
item.description: The consequences are mounting. Anthropic CFO Krishna Rao warned in court filings that the government's actions could cut the company's 2026 revenue by "several billion dollars". The company could lose 50% to 100% of revenue from defense contractors and Pentagon-linked clients, according to Rao. Anthropic's head of public sector, Thiyagu Ramasamy, estimated an immediate loss of over $150 million...

item.title: Iran's president's son says new leader is 'safe and sound' amid injury reports
item.description: Despite his appointment, Iranian state media has relied on archival footage and AI-generated imagery to present the new leader to the public, according to Iran International. The only recording attributed to him is an older clip announcing the cancellation of his religious classes. Crowds of supporters have gathered in cities across Iran to pledge allegiance, but Khamenei himself has been...

item.title: Trump to visit Ohio, Kentucky to sell economic message and campaign against Massie
item.description: The two-stop swing serves as an early test of whether Trump can simultaneously maintain a credible economic message and enforce party discipline ahead of November's midterm elections. Some Republicans are skeptical his political machine can unseat Massie, who claims a 17-point lead in internal polling and has built a loyal following in the district over seven terms. Gallrein, for his part, has...

item.title: Von der Leyen calls return to Russian energy 'a strategic blunder' amid Iran war
item.description: Von der Leyen's speech capped a week in which she also declared Europe's abandonment of nuclear power a "strategic mistake" at the IAEA Nuclear Energy Summit in Paris, announcing a €200 million guarantee to support investment in small modular reactors. The Commission simultaneously unveiled a clean energy investment strategy aimed at accelerating the deployment of renewables and nuclear to...

item.title: UN Security Council to vote on resolution demanding Iran halt Gulf attacks
item.description: Whether the resolution can pass remains uncertain, given potential vetoes from Russia or China. Meanwhile, U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the war would not end until the enemy is "totally and decisively" defeated, even as Trump projected optimism. With Iran retaining an estimated 80% of its smaller naval vessels and mine-laying capacity, and commercial shipping still largely frozen in...

item.title: Asian stocks rally as IEA mulls record oil reserve release
item.description: The conflict, which began on February 28 with joint U.S.-Israeli airstrikes that killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, has upended the global energy outlook. U.S. gasoline prices have jumped roughly 50 cents in a week, and analysts warn they could reach a $4 national average if oil remains elevated. The specter of stagflation — stagnant growth paired with persistent inflation — now hangs...

item.title: US gas prices top $3.50 as Iran war disrupts Strait of Hormuz oil flow
item.description: The price spike has hit states unevenly. California leads the nation at $5.29 per gallon, followed by Washington at $4.69, Hawaii at $4.59, and Nevada at $4.30, according to AAA data. Governor Gavin Newsom noted that California's average had stayed below $5 for nearly two years before the conflict pushed it past that threshold. Diesel prices have climbed even faster, with the national average...

item.title: New AI tools compress months of drug and protein research into days
item.description: Scientists at Berkeley Lab's Joint BioEnergy Institute built a high-throughput platform capable of testing thousands of plant gene switches — the DNA sequences that control when genes turn on and off — in a single experiment. The tool addresses a bottleneck in plant synthetic biology: while CRISPR can edit genes with precision, identifying which regulatory elements to target has remained slow...

item.title: Trial opens for 4 men charged in Haiti president's assassination
item.description: A fifth defendant, Haitian American pastor Christian Sanon, will face trial separately after undergoing surgery for colon cancer. Of the original 11 defendants indicted in the U.S., five have already pleaded guilty and received life sentences, while a sixth was sentenced to nine years for supplying body armor. Another 20 people, including 17 Colombian soldiers and three Haitian officials, face...

item.title: AG Bondi relocates to military base amid cartel, Epstein threats
item.description: Former Defense Secretary Leon Panetta told The New York Times that if today's officials face elevated threats, the government could build new housing rather than displacing military families. While some precedent exists — Congress authorized the vice president's residence at the Naval Observatory in 1974 — observers note it is unusual for so many civilian appointees to occupy military quarters...

item.title: Experts accuse Bukele's government of crimes against humanity
item.description:
Salvadoran Public Defender General Rene Escobar rejected the accusations, stating the administration opposes any policy involving "forced disappearance, torture, sexual violence, or arbitrary executions." Government figures show homicides have fallen by more than 90 percent during Bukele's presidency.
Yet the report argues the emergency measures have long outlived their stated purpose. "Its...

item.title: Fuller and Harris head to Georgia runoff for Greene seat
item.description:
Harris faces long odds in the runoff. Trump carried the district by 37 points in 2024, and Greene won it with roughly 64% of the vote that same year. But Democrats see reason for optimism. Harris previously ran against Greene in 2024, earning about 36% of the district vote, and his first-place finish Tuesday suggested that a fractured Republican electorate may have worked in his favor.
Harris...

item.title: Pentagon orders removal of Anthropic AI from military systems
item.description: The fallout extends well beyond Anthropic. Palantir Technologies, whose Maven Smart Systems platform relies on Claude for intelligence analysis and weapons targeting, faces the task of replacing the AI model and rebuilding portions of its software — a process sources say could take months. Anthropic executives have said the blacklisting could cut the company's 2026 revenue by billions of...

item.title: Iran launches drone strikes on Haifa refinery in second attack in three days
item.description: The tit-for-tat strikes on energy infrastructure are part of what analysts describe as "Phase II" of a broader military confrontation that erupted on February 28 when the United States and Israel launched joint strikes on Tehran and other Iranian cities. Those initial attacks killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei along with family members, military commanders, and civilians, according to...
